subject: Re: Help with MTools setup for multiple sources
body:
Hi Lucas,

Yeah this is definitely an issue that can come up and is irritating - we
should fix it :-) have made an issue
<https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/309> to track.

I think the clearest path forwards for you is to append something unique to
each dataset to both your tomostar and xml files, then modify rlnTomoName
(or rlnMicrographName, whichever is present) in your particle star files to
include this.

I don't foresee any problems with this strategy but let me know if you run
into issues...

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 2 Dec 2024 at 09:40, Lucas Morley <lmorley1100@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi,
>
> I have processed 3 different data sets collected on different days using
> Warp. Dynamo, and Relion.
>
> I have the directory structure set up where there is a base directory and
> within the base directory each dataset is processed in a separate directory
> e.g. set_1, set_2, set_3. Additionally this is where I have a dynamo
> directory and relion directory.
>
> Within each set_# the data was processed similar to the tutorial using
> names like warp_tiltseries.settings etc. Additionally, the corresponding
> mdocs were copied into each set_1/mdoc directory with the names they had
> when they were collected e.g. TS1.mrc.mdoc. This also means that there are
> some TS with the same name across the datasets. This has not caused any
> issues so far because the file paths are different so for instance Relion
> knows the difference between for example:
>
> ../set_1/warp_tiltseries/subtomo/TS35/TS35_0000000_10.00A.mrc
> ../set_2/warp_tiltseries/subtomo/TS35/TS35_0000000_10.00A.mrc
>
> This has not caused an issues until now when I am trying to use M. I
> created sources for each different data set giving them each a different
> name. However, when I create the species I get the following error:
>
> Parsing particle table... 31 image names are ambiguous between selected
> data sources.
>
> I was thinking of appending set_# to the beginning of each TS but since I
> have processed the data a fair bit now I am unsure if there is any way to
> do this without starting over since these names are referenced in numerous
> places.
>
> Is there any way for M to tell the differences between these files?
>
> Thanks for the help,
> Lucas
>
> --

> .
>
